name: Apple-Pear Tart with Cornmeal Crust
recipeIngredient: Heavy cream or half-and-half: 1/3 cup + 1 tablespoon
Cranberries (fresh or frozen): 1/2 cup, more as desired
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the pastry: Combine cornmeal, flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor with a steel blade. Cut cold butter into thin slices while still cold, then add to the food processor. Pulse until evenly distributed and mixture resembles coarse meal. Add egg and pulse once or twice until just incorporated. Add enough cream to bring dough together. Remove dough from food processor and press into one mass.
2. Divide dough into two pieces (about two-thirds and one-third). Form each into a ball, flatten into a thick disk, and roll between 2 sheets of wax paper until about 1/8 inch thick. Place the larger circle in a 9- or 10-inch pie pan and trim edges. Cut the smaller circle into strips about 1/2 inch wide.
3. Preheat oven to 375F (190C).
4. Make the filling: Slice apples and pear thinly, transfer to a bowl, and drizzle with lemon juice and zest, tossing to coat. Combine cornstarch, cinnamon, nutmeg, sugars, and salt in a bowl, sprinkle over fruit, and toss to coat.
5. Arrange fruit in crust so skins show, sprinkle cranberries on top, and arrange dough strips in criss-cross pattern to form lattice topping.
6. Place filled tart on baking tray and bake in lower half of oven for about 40 minutes, or until light golden and edges are set. Cool at least 15 minutes before slicing tart. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Wine Suggestions:
- Beginning of the day
- End of the day
- Beginning of the meal
- End of the meal
All these options suggest pouring and enjoying Champagne. A bottle of Moët & Chandon White Star is recommended.
Yield: 1 10-inch tart
PER serving: 430 CAL; 6 G PROT; 17 G TOTAL FAT (10 SAT. FAT); 65 G CARB.; 80 MG CHOL; 240 MG SOD.; 5 G FIBER.
